content: Defines reward hacking in the context of reinforcement learning (RL)
Discusses safety concerns in AI systems (autonomous vehicles, algorithmic trading, content recommendation systems)
Introduces the challenge of aligning AI systems with user intentions
Mentions approaches to specify desirable behaviors
Raises the question: When is it safe to optimize a proxy?
Considers when optimizing a proxy could lead to worse behavior
Discusses theoretical results about hackable and unhackable reward function pairs
Formal definition of hackable reward function pairs
Theoretical results about unhackable reward function pairs
Discussion of simplification as an asymmetric special case
Examples showing that natural ways of simplifying reward functions often fail
